model_explanation.title: Model Explanation
model_explanation.description: Match outcomes are predicted using a Random Forest Regressor trained separately for each season. The model predicts full-time home and away goals, from which the result and Superbru score are derived. It was selected after comparing multiple regressors for its ability to capture non-linear feature interactions without heavy manual tuning.

points.title: Elo Ratings
subpoints: Each team carries a running Elo rating updated after every match (K=30).
Home teams receive a +100 advantage. At each new season, ratings regress 20% toward the 1500 base to reflect squad turnover.
The pre-match Elo for home (elo_h) and away (elo_a) teams are the model's strongest signals of relative team strength.

points.title: Rolling Shooting Stats (3-game window)
subpoints: Shots, shots on target, goals, and other shooting metrics are averaged over the team's last 3 matches.
Separate rolling features are computed for the home team (_h suffix) and the away team (_a suffix).
A left-closed window ensures only pre-match data is used, preventing data leakage.

points.title: Rolling xG (Expected Goals)
subpoints: xG for and xG against are averaged over the last 3 matches for each team (xg_rolling_h/a, xg_against_rolling_h/a).
Sourced from FBref shooting CSVs. Falls back to 0 for seasons before xG data was tracked (pre-2017).

points.title: Head-to-Head History
subpoints: Average goals scored and conceded in the last 5 meetings between the two teams (both home and away directions).
Defaults to the league average (1.5) when no H2H history exists.

points.title: Previous Season Standings
subpoints: Each team's final position, goals for, goals against, and goal difference from the prior season are included (pos/gf/ga/gd_last_season_h/a).
Newly promoted teams that were not in the EPL the prior season receive default values (rank 18, league-average goals).

points.title: Days Rest
subpoints: The number of days since each team's previous match (days_rest_h, days_rest_a).
Captures fatigue and fixture congestion effects, particularly relevant mid-season.

points.title: Cumulative Season Points & PPG
subpoints: Points accumulated so far in the current season for each team (cum_pts_h, cum_pts_a).
Rolling 3-game points per game (ppg_rolling_h/a) captures recent form independently of the overall season tally.

points.title: Match Context
subpoints: Team names and venue are label-encoded as categorical features.

model_workflow.title: How It Works
model_workflow.description: The pipeline runs end-to-end from raw data to per-matchweek predictions. Each season uses its own trained model so the feature distributions are kept in-sample.

steps.title: Data Collection
substeps: Historical match results are scraped from football-data.co.uk and cached in SQLite.
Shooting and xG stats are scraped from FBref and saved as per-team CSVs.
Previous season standings are read from a consolidated 2000–2025 CSV.

steps.title: Feature Engineering
substeps: Elo ratings are computed chronologically across all seasons with a season-start regression.
Rolling shooting stats, rolling xG, PPG, days rest, H2H averages, and prior-season standings are all added as features.
Categorical variables (team names, venue) are label-encoded.

steps.title: Model Training
substeps: A separate RandomForestRegressor is trained per season on all prior seasons' data.
Hyperparameters are tuned via a dedicated script (tune_random_forest.py) using cross-validation.
Trained models and scalers are persisted with joblib for fast prediction serving.

steps.title: Prediction & Scoring
substeps: For upcoming fixtures, the same feature pipeline runs with known pre-match values; unplayed match stats are imputed from rolling averages.
Predicted home and away goals are rounded to the nearest integer to produce a scoreline.
The result (H/D/A) and Superbru points are derived from the predicted scoreline.

steps.title: Daily Updates
substeps: A daily script (daily_predict_and_submit.py) refreshes predictions for the current matchweek.
A second script (daily_update_results.py) pulls actual results and updates Superbru scoring.
